offer.offerOther: weekly grocery boxes and pop-up distributions
offer.deliveryOther: weekly boxes and local pop-up distributions

transformation.afterOther: a recognized hub neighbors know how to support
transformation.mechanismOther: consistent distributions and public impact reporting

step2.customerArchetype: Donors, volunteers, and municipal partners who want impact they can see
step2.painPoints: Fatigue with opaque charities; need to trust where money and time go
step2.desiredOutcomes: Clear stories, easy ways to help, and dignity-forward messaging for participants

step4.missionStatement: Neighbors feeding neighbors — with transparency and respect at every step.

step5.originSummary: Started as a church basement table during the pandemic; demand never went back down.
step5.motivation: Nobody should have to choose between rent and dinner in this zip code.

competitors: Informal mutual aid with no public face
step7.differentiation: Local intake, local volunteers, and reporting that names real households helped — not aggregate press releases.
